title: Spatial Validation of CAF2 Fibroblasts in Hepatocellular Carcinoma Using Imaging Mass Cytometry

sample_info.sample_type: HCC liver tissue microarray cores (tumour and non-tumour ROIs across two TMAs, HCC3 and HCC4); ~1.27 million segmented cells over 88 shared ROIs

analytical_questions.primary_question: Can the simpleSeg and CellProfiler pipelines be reliably joined on object ID so that validated per-cell intensities and spatial coordinates can be combined for spatial analysis of CAF2 fibroblasts in HCC?
other_questions: Is the per-core file structure, ROI metadata, and object ID mapping consistent across segmentation masks, intensity files, and clinical metadata?
Do simpleSeg and CellProfiler produce consistent per-cell marker intensities?
What explains the aSMA discrepancy between the two pipelines?
Is there evidence of over-segmentation in the provided segmentation masks?
Are the FlowJo manual-gating cell type annotations biologically coherent?
Do cell type proportions differ between tumour and non-tumour tissue?

qc.qc_question: Are all images, masks, and clinical metadata complete and correctly structured?
qc.qc_method: file audit; 91 images, 89 masks, 88 shared ROIs; image/mask alignment and metadata checks

qc.qc_question: Are the segmentation masks correctly registered to the raw images?
qc.qc_method: plotPixels mask overlays on DNA1/CD45/CK8 for representative ROIs

qc.qc_question: Do simpleSeg and CellProfiler measure the same cells with consistent intensities?
qc.qc_method: per-marker Pearson correlation (>0.9999 for all markers except aSMA, r = 0.083)

qc.qc_question: Is there evidence of over-segmentation in the masks?
qc.qc_method: per-ROI cell area distribution vs 50 µm² threshold; cell count vs median area scatter

qc.qc_question: Are marker distributions consistent across ROIs after normalisation?
qc.qc_method: per-ROI density plots before/after trim99+mean+PC1 normalisation

qc.qc_question: Are the cell type annotations biologically coherent?
qc.qc_method: grouped marker expression heatmap, UMAP, and per-ROI cell type composition

primary_methods: cell type annotation via manual gating
differential cell type composition (linear mixed effects)
